meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing to overcome the no portfolio trap. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to build real-world value.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no portfolio' problem, build a functional SaaS pricing engine. Recruiters value projects that demonstrate business logic, not just UI. By integrating a dynamic pricing model, you prove you can handle complex state management and financial calculations, turning a blank resume into a professional, revenue-ready demonstration of technical competence.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank GitHub profile, the silence of 'no commits' echoing louder than your ambition. You have the skills, but the industry doesn't see them yet. The gap between a tutorial-follower and an engineer isn't just code; it's the ability to translate business requirements into working software.

We are going to bridge that gap. By building a robust, production-grade SaaS pricing module, you stop being a student and start being a developer who understands the heartbeat of a business. This isn't just a project; it's the artifact that proves you can build, scale, and monetize.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Architecture
steps.description: Establish the data model for your pricing tiers (Free, Pro, Enterprise). This step ensures your schema can handle future feature gating.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing model including tiered billing, feature arrays, and currency handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize pricing schema using the provided Antigravity spec'

steps.title: Build the Pricing Logic Engine
steps.description: Develop the core function that calculates costs based on user usage. This shows you can handle algorithmic logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a utility function that calculates monthly recurring revenue based on tier selection and active user seat count.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement the pricing calculation engine in src/utils/pricing.ts'

steps.title: Implement State Management
steps.description: Ensure the UI reflects the pricing state accurately. This demonstrates proficiency in modern frontend frameworks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React hook that manages the state of selected pricing tiers and updates the total cost in real-time.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add usePricing hook to the project'

steps.title: Integrate Mock Payment Gateway
steps.description: Simulate the checkout process. Adding a payment layer creates a high-fidelity project that stands out.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a secure-looking mock checkout component that validates the selected pricing plan before triggering a success state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create Checkout component with validation logic'

steps.title: Document for Deployment
steps.description: Write technical documentation for your project. Documentation is the hallmark of a senior developer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a README.md file that explains the architecture, pricing logic, and how to extend this project.

pro_tips: Always include a 'Cost Per User' breakdown in your UI; it demonstrates you understand SaaS unit economics.
Use TypeScript interfaces for pricing constants to prevent silent bugs during billing calculations.
Add a 'Toggle Annual/Monthly' feature early; it shows you can handle complex UI state transitions.
Structure your code so that features are array-based objects, allowing for instant updates to tiers without changing core logic.
Treat your README as a sales pitch to recruiters; highlight the 'Why' behind your technical decisions, not just the 'How'.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel, share the live link on LinkedIn, and start networking with other builders to iterate on your features.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-level project that sets you apart from every other applicant. Don't stop here. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
